title: Overall stock-consumption summary conflates different sections at same length
description: The overall stock-bar consumption roll-up groups purely by stock_length_mm, so distinct sections at the same length are merged into one line (e.g. 24 angle + 24 channel @12200 shows as '48@12200'). This is wrong for procurement. Fix: reshape the overall stock_consumption field to be section-major (by_section: [{designation, consumption}]), keeping distinct profiles separate. Update CSV + XLSX renderers. Downstream, the portal HTML builder (platform-portal/apps/nesting) should consume the field instead of recomputing locally (kills a duplicate copy). Per-section field unchanged. Relates to epic platform-portal-6gr.

title: CLAUDE.md still describes solver as two-phase; it is now three-phase
description: The 'Solver algorithm (beam_nesting.py)' section in CLAUDE.md describes a two-phase CP-SAT approach (Phase 1 maximise placement, Phase 2 minimise waste). The code is now three-phase when pack_tight is true (the default): Phase 1 maximise placement, Phase 2 minimise bar count, Phase 3 minimise weighted waste (scrap[j] * (num_bins - j)) to concentrate offcut onto the last bar. pack_tight=false falls back to the original two-phase (placement then minimise total waste). Update the CLAUDE.md section to match. Recent commits: 9e7905b (three-phase), f5f167d (weighted Phase 3). The wiki dev page 'Nesting Service: Technical Overview' already documents the current three-phase behaviour and can be used as the source.
close_reason: Updated CLAUDE.md solver section to describe the current three-phase approach (placement -> bar count -> weighted waste), plus the pack_tight=false two-phase fallback; also fixed the project-structure comment. Matches beam_nesting.py and the wiki dev page.

title: Nesting report: add stock-bar consumption summary (N @ length)
description:
Report should summarise stock bars consumed, grouped by length — e.g. `4 beams: 2@12200, 2@10000` — per section plus an overall total. Data already exists (stock_length_mm per bar), so it's a roll-up/formatting addition.

## Where

- pss-nesting-service — src/services/cutting-list.ts (JSON/CSV/XLSX)
- pss-document-service — PDF template

Lives in the shared report generator → benefits both BOM-quote and IFC/CAD nests.

## Worked example (0005)

`OVERALL 120 beams: 72@14000, 48@12000`
close_reason: Implemented stock-bar consumption roll-up grouped by length, longest first (e.g. 120 bars: 72@14000, 48@12000). nesting-service: new StockConsumption type; computed per-section and overall in toCuttingList; surfaced in JSON, CSV, and XLSX. document-service: rendered overall line under KPI cards plus a per-section line in the cutting-list PDF. Verified against the 0005 worked example; both repos typecheck; CSV/XLSX/PDF generate. Labelled bars not beams for consistency with the existing report. Changes uncommitted; doc-service part needs its own commit/deploy.

title: Portal Rerun loses per-piece member traceability
description:
Rerun re-solves from the stored request_payload (consolidated {length, qty} cut rows), which has no per-piece member field — so a Rerun's cutting list drops ref_id/member/parent. The BOM push tool submits per-piece items, so the initial View is traceable (e.g. DB100), but a portal Rerun loses it.

## Options

- **Option 1 — widen the pipeline:** carry member/parent on cut rows through the portal form + request_payload. Touches apps/nesting/NestingPage.tsx, SavedPayload, item building.
- **Option 2 — document the workaround:** leave as-is; a traceable list requires a re-push through the BOM tool.

## Workaround today

Re-push via bom_poc.portal_import.

## Notes

Cross-repo: the fix (Option 1) lands in platform-portal (apps/nesting), not this service. Tracked here because it surfaces in the nesting cutting-list output. Related platform-portal issue: platform-portal-0my.
